# Recover lost comment threads

77 comment threads were created via `forge-mcp` `create_comment` but their refs were clobbered by concurrent writers (7 parallel agents).
The commit objects are still in the ODB — they just need refs pointed at them, then the indexes rebuilt.

## Step 1: Restore thread refs

For each `(thread_id, commit_oid)` pair below, create the ref:

```text
git update-ref refs/forge/comments/<thread_id> <commit_oid>
```

Here are all 77 pairs (`thread_id commit_oid`):

## Step 2: Rebuild indexes

After all refs are restored, run `forge reindex` (or the equivalent CLI command) to rebuild `refs/forge/index/comments-by-object` and `refs/forge/index/comments-by-comment` from the now-reachable thread refs.

If there's no CLI reindex command, call `rebuild_comments_index` via the MCP or write a short script that does:

```rust
git_forge::comment::rebuild_comments_index(&repo)?;
```

## Step 3: Verify

```sh
# Should list 77 thread refs (plus any pre-existing ones)
git for-each-ref 'refs/forge/comments/' --format='%(refname)' | wc -l

# Should show all anchor OIDs indexed
git ls-tree -r refs/forge/index/comments-by-object

# Should list all comments
forge comment list --all
```

## Context

The root cause is a TOCTOU race in `create_thread` → `index_add_anchor`: each writer reads the current index tree, inserts its entry, and force-updates the ref.
With 7 concurrent writers, the last one to commit wins and the other 6 entries are lost.
The thread refs (`refs/forge/comments/<uuid>`) are independent so they _should_ survive — but in this case something (likely `rebuild` calls triggered by failures) also clobbered them.
